summary: Completed testing of Japanese language toggle feature. Core functionality works correctly but identified critical navigation issue and missing translations in login form.

critical_bugs.issue: Language toggle flags are not visible on landing page ('/') because Navigation component returns null for NO_NAV_PAGES
critical_bugs.impact: Users cannot switch languages on the main landing page, violating requirement for 'consistent navigation across pages'
critical_bugs.details: Landing page ('/') is in NO_NAV_PAGES array, causing Navigation to return null and hide language toggle

ui_bugs.issue: Login form labels (Username, Password) are not translated to Japanese
ui_bugs.impact: Form labels remain in English even when Japanese language is selected
ui_bugs.details: Component does not import or use useTranslation hook, labels are hardcoded instead of using t('login.username') and t('login.password')

passed_tests: Language toggle flags appear correctly in navigation on pages where navigation is shown (login page)
Clicking Japanese flag changes UI text to Japanese (landing page content translates properly)
Clicking British flag changes UI text back to English
Language preference is persisted in localStorage correctly
Landing page shows translated Japanese content when Japanese is selected
Visual state of flag buttons works correctly (active/inactive styling)
HTML lang attribute changes correctly between 'en' and 'ja'
Language preference persists after page refresh and navigation
action_item_for_main_agent: 1. CRITICAL: Add language toggle to landing page navigation - modify Navigation.js to show at least language toggle on landing page, or add LanguageToggle component directly to Landing.js. 2. MEDIUM: Fix ModeratorLogin.js to use useTranslation hook for form labels (import useTranslation and replace hardcoded 'Username'/'Password' with t('login.username')/t('login.password'))
success_percentage: Backend: N/A (frontend-only testing), Frontend: 67% (core functionality works but missing on landing page)
